description: This map displays an Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) interferogram overlaid on mountainous terrain, illustrating ground deformation alongside areas of decorrelation and atmospheric effects. A compass rose indicating North is in the top-left corner, and a scale bar of "20 km" is in the top-right. A prominent central-right feature shows a bullseye-like pattern of concentric, multi-coloured fringes (blue, green, yellow, red), representing significant ground deformation. Several areas are explicitly labelled "decorrelation" (top-centre, mid-right, mid-bottom), which appear as noisy, grey textures lacking coherent phase patterns. In the bottom-left, a distinct band of blue, yellow, and red patterns is labelled with arrows as "atmospheric effects," indicating phase shifts due to atmospheric conditions. A colour bar at the bottom indicates the interferometric phase range from -π (dark blue) through 0 (green/yellow) to +π (red). The underlying terrain is depicted in shades of grey.
